heading: Requirement to buy certain items related to national security interests from American sources; exceptions
label: 453b Requirement to buy certain items related to national security interests from American sources; exceptions
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009, and not as part of the Homeland Security Act of 2002 which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: a Requirement
children.text: Except as provided in subsections (c) through (g), funds appropriated or otherwise available to the Department of Homeland Security may not be used for the procurement of an item described in subsection (b) if the item is not grown, reprocessed, reused, or produced in the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: clothing and the materials and components thereof, other than sensors, electronics, or other items added to, and not normally associated with, clothing (and the materials and components thereof);

children.label: B
children.text: tents, tarpaulins, covers, textile belts, bags, protective equipment (including but not limited to body armor), sleep systems, load carrying equipment (including but not limited to fieldpacks), textile marine equipment, parachutes, or bandages;

children.label: C
children.text: cotton and other natural fiber products, woven silk or woven silk blends, spun silk yarn for cartridge cloth, synthetic fabric or coated synthetic fabric (including all textile fibers and yarns that are for use in such fabrics), canvas products, or wool (whether in the form of fiber or yarn or contained in fabrics, materials, or manufactured articles); or

children.label: D
children.text: any item of individual equipment manufactured from or containing such fibers, yarns, fabrics, or materials.

children.heading: Availability exception
children.label: c Availability exception
children.text: Subsection (a) does not apply to the extent that the Secretary of Homeland Security determines that satisfactory quality and sufficient quantity of any such article or item described in subsection (b)(1) grown, reprocessed, reused, or produced in the United States cannot be procured as and when needed at United States market prices. This section is not applicable to covered items that are, or include, materials determined to be non-available in accordance with Federal Acquisition Regulation 25.104 Nonavailable Articles.

children.heading: De minimis exception
children.label: d De minimis exception
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (a), the Secretary of Homeland Security may accept delivery of an item covered by subsection (b) that contains non-compliant fibers if the total value of non-compliant fibers contained in the end item does not exceed 10 percent of the total purchase price of the end item.

children.heading: Exception for certain procurements outside the United States
children.label: e Exception for certain procurements outside the United States

children.text: Procurements by vessels in foreign waters.

children.heading: Exception for small purchases
children.label: f Exception for small purchases
children.text: Subsection (a) does not apply to purchases for amounts not greater than the simplified acquisition threshold referred to in .section 3205 of title 10

children.heading: Applicability to contracts and subcontracts for procurement of commercial products
children.label: g Applicability to contracts and subcontracts for procurement of commercial products
children.text: This section is applicable to contracts and subcontracts for the procurement of commercial products notwithstanding , with the exception of commercial products listed under subsections (b)(1)(C) and (b)(1)(D) above. For the purposes of this section, “commercial product” shall be as defined in .section 1906 of title 41section 103 of title 41

children.heading: Geographic coverage
children.label: h Geographic coverage
children.text: In this section, the term “United States” includes the possessions of the United States.

children.heading: Notification required within 7 days after contract award if certain exceptions applied
children.label: i Notification required within 7 days after contract award if certain exceptions applied
children.text: In the case of any contract for the procurement of an item described in subsection (b)(1), if the Secretary of Homeland Security applies an exception set forth in subsection (c) with respect to that contract, the Secretary shall, not later than 7 days after the award of the contract, post a notification that the exception has been applied on the Internet site maintained by the General Services Administration known as FedBizOps.gov (or any successor site).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security shall ensure that each member of the acquisition workforce in the Department of Homeland Security who participates personally and substantially in the acquisition of textiles on a regular basis receives training during fiscal year 2009 on the requirements of this section and the regulations implementing this section.

children.heading: Inclusion of information in new training programs
children.label: 2 Inclusion of information in new training programs
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that any training program for the acquisition workforce developed or implemented after , includes comprehensive information on the requirements described in paragraph (1).February 17, 2009

children.heading: Consistency with international agreements
children.label: k Consistency with international agreements
children.text: This section shall be applied in a manner consistent with United States obligations under international agreements.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: l Effective date
children.text: This section applies with respect to contracts entered into by the Department of Homeland Security 180 days after .February 17, 2009
